sections.paragraph:
ἀχώριστος, ον (s. χωρίζω; X., Pla. et al.; ApcMos 42; Just., D. 128, 3) inseparable w. gen. foll. (Cornutus 14 p. 15, 14; Philo, Gig. 48) ἀ. θεοῦ (Lghtf. suggests omission of θεοῦ w. the Armenian vers.) Ἰ. Χ. inseparable fr. our God Jesus Christ ITr 7:1 (cp. Herm. Wr. 2, 16 τὸ ἀγαθὸν ἀ. τοῦ θεοῦ; Sextus 423; Jos., Ant. 9, 273).—DELG s.v. χώρα.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

gen. gen. = genitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Cornutus Cornutus , I a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

vers. vers. = version(s)

  1. fr. = from

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 161
